title: Sidrona Diath

{{Quote|Blessed be your going out from us.|translated=1|Sidrona Diath, speaking in [[Tusken/Legends|Tusken]]|<ref name="outlander">[[Star Wars (1998) 7|''Star Wars'' (1998) 7]]</ref>}}
'''Sidrona Diath''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] up until his [[Death/Legends|death]] in the [[Battle of Basilisk]] in [[4017 BBY]].

==Biography==
===Service to the Republic===
{{Quote|Master Diath was a good man and a revered leader of Tatooine who fought valiantly before his fall in the Battle of Basilisk some 4,000 years ago. He also fathered Jedi Knight Dace Diath, a hero in the Sith War.|[[Tatooine Coalition for the Preservation of the Prominent Past]]|<ref name="SWG:DoaAJG" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]],<ref name="SoT">''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]''</ref> Sidrona Diath was born on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]<ref name="outlander" /> during the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]'s early period of colonization. Receiving [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], Diath eventually attained the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. During this time, Diath fathered a son named [[Dace Diath|Dace]], whom he raised on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] in the ways of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The younger Diath would eventually attain the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and study the ways of the Force on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]].<ref name="SoT">''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]''</ref> Sidrona Diath uttered the immortal words "Koroght gaghgt Takt" in [[Tusken/Legends|Tusken]] to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], with the nearest [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] translation was "Blessed be your going out from us."<ref name="outlander" />

In [[4017 BBY]],<ref name="Warfare" /> Master Sidrona Diath took to the front lines of [[Basilisk]] to do [[Battle of Basilisk|battle]]<ref name="Masters" /> with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]].<ref name="Warfare" /> During the fighting, Diath was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the enemy.<ref name="Masters" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|In our city lies an ancient grave site honoring Jedi Master Sidrona Diath. Sadly, the monument was senselessly defaced by a local group of Jedi haters. It was probably done to curry favor with the local Imperials.|Tatooine Coalition for the Preservation of the Prominent Past|<ref name="SWG:DoaAJG" />}}
Sidrona Diath's body was later returned to his homeworld Tatooine and buried there.<ref name="SWG:DoaAJG" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], several millennium after Diath's death, members of the Jedi High Council recalled the famed Jedi Master's last words spoken to members of the Council of the era: "Koroght gaghgt Takt." Master [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] reflected on these words during a discussion of the famed Jedi-turned-[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken]] [[Sharad Hett]].<ref name="outlander" />

Master Diath was still revered by the inhabitants of Tatooine centuries after his death.<ref name="SWG:DoaAJG" /><ref name="SWG:DS">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=terminal||Diath's Shadow}} on Tatooine</ref> Some of his history would be chronicled by [[Alaikai Trotter]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="SWG:DS" /> However, in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Diath's ancient grave site was defaced by an [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] group. Although Jedi supporters were threatened by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Tatooine Coalition for the Preservation of the Prominent Past]] recruited a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to hunt and fight the defilers.<ref name="SWG:DoaAJG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly states that Sidrona Diath was killed by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] at the Battle of Basilisk.
